prompt:
A CampusOps Fulfillment & Operations platform capable of running entirely on a disconnected local network supports district staff in managing classroom operations data alongside on-premise logistics fulfillment for devices, materials, and lost-and-found returns. Primary roles include Administrator (system setup and security), Operations Manager (warehouses, carriers, and service policies), Classroom Supervisor (real-time monitoring and incident closure), Customer Service Agent (tickets and compensation), and Auditor (read-only review of sensitive actions). The Vue.js web console provides a real-time dashboard that highlights classroom online status, a recognition confidence indicator, and an anomaly event stream that operators can acknowledge, assign, and resolve with a required resolution note. A separate parking operations view shows available-space counts, turnover (entries/hour), and exception alerts such as no plate captured, overtime, unsettled session, duplicate plate, or inconsistent entry/exit; each alert must be closed within 15 minutes or escalates to a supervisor queue. Users can configure shipping fee templates by weight (lb), item count, region, and tier (e.g., a $6.95 base fee up to 2 lb plus $1.25 per additional lb, with Alaska/Hawaii surcharges), define delivery zones and non-serviceable ZIP codes, and maintain warehouses and carrier options. A standardized after-sales ticketing flow supports delay, dispute, and lost item cases with evidence upload (photo and text), automated compensation suggestions (e.g., $10.00 credit for delivery over 48 hours late, capped at $50.00 per ticket) plus manual approval, and SLA timers visible on the ticket timeline. Membership management allows tiered benefits and growth points, with member-only pricing and coupons applied at checkout-like fulfillment requests; stored value is optional and, if enabled, supports top-up and spending within the platform UI with clear balance feedback and printable receipts.

The backend uses Express to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the Vue.js frontend over the local network, with consistent idempotency keys for retries on create/update operations and a job monitor for background tasks such as bulk imports and shipment status sync. MySQL persists master data (students, classes, departments, courses, semesters) with uniqueness and referential integrity enforced, plus bulk import/export from Excel/CSV with row-level validation and an error report downloadable for correction. Logistics data models link shipment records to parcels and to after-sales tickets so a single parcel can be referenced during disputes; tracking numbers are stored and shipment-tracking sync APIs are implemented as internal connectors to on-prem carrier systems only, with no internet dependency. Observability is built into the admin console using locally collected runtime metrics (p95 latency, CPU/GPU utilization if present, and error rate), searchable application logs with retention of 30 days, and configurable thresholds that trigger on-screen alerts and optional local email-free notifications (e.g., banner and audible workstation alert). File handling uses local object storage on a mounted disk path, with image compression/cropping and basic content safeguards limited to format/size checks and perceptual hashing for duplicate detection; uploads are capped at 10 MB per file and only JPEG/PNG are accepted. Security requires username/password login with salted hashing, role-based access control, field-level masking for personally identifiable information, AES-256 encryption for stored value balances and audit logs at rest, TLS for transport even on LAN, API signing for privileged integrations, rate limiting and circuit breakers to prevent overload, and scheduled backups with tested restore procedures (daily full backup, 14-day retention) to a separate local disk.
